messages.author: Michael (M7NMB)
messages.text: Hope to be setting up a RAK Solar repeater as soon as the funds allow me to get all the gear together... Managed to get out mobile with the Heltec V3 and jumped on a repeater local to me in SG2. Anyway opening up a new chan for Hertfordshire..... p.s. im new to this also couldnt find a herts chan so created this one.

messages.author: Yano
messages.text: Hi Scorpion. You can only mount an antenna via its metal base to the mast. Mounting it like you have there will de-tune the antenna and make it practically unusable. The rest of the antenna, the grey bit, should be above the metal mast. The coax should be as short as possible..
messages.content_html: Hi Scorpion. You can only mount an antenna via its metal base to the mast. Mounting it like you have there will de-tune the antenna and make it practically unusable. The rest of the antenna, the grey bit, should be above the metal mast. The coax should be as short as possible..
messages.reactions_html: 1

Michael (M7NMB)
Repeater is now up not very high but its up for now...

messages.author: Michael (M7NMB)
messages.text: ok so i dont think my repeater is getting out to local repeaters as only seeing Rx flood messages and none of my Tx messages are being acknowledged or show hops etc... not much i can do for the mo unless someone else local adds a new repeater that bridges any gaps there may be.
messages.content_html: ok so i dont think my repeater is getting out to local repeaters as only seeing Rx flood messages and none of my Tx messages are being acknowledged or show hops etc... not much i can do for the mo unless someone else local adds a new repeater that bridges any gaps there may be.

messages.author: Michael (M7NMB)
messages.text: Am i iright in thinking the way this system works is nodes connect to repeaters and repeaters connect to other repeaters to create the mesh. A stable constant connection between repeaters is needed for live comms irregular connections are ok for "flood" messages which is why im seeing those and nothing else as they send as and when they can,... (edited)
messages.content_html: Am i iright in thinking the way this system works is nodes connect to repeaters and repeaters connect to other repeaters to create the mesh. A stable constant connection between repeaters is needed for live comms irregular connections are ok for "flood" messages which is why im seeing those and nothing else as they send as and when they can,... (edited)

andykirby
It might just be that homebase antenna you are using isnt great or not high enough. I could always hit handhelds in stevenage on 2m/70cm so I would have thought you'd get my hertford omni station from here. (edited)
